meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Design with AI to Solve Basic App Limits
content.seo_description: Break through development plateaus. Use this advanced checklist to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code for high-end, scalable SaaS architecture.
content.answer_block: Stop building basic CRUD apps. To scale, integrate Antigravity for rapid UI/UX structural generation and Claude Code for iterative, context-aware logic implementation. By decoupling architectural design from manual boilerplate coding, you shift from a 'code monkey' to a high-leverage SaaS architect, delivering premium, production-ready features in a fraction of the time.
content.intro:
You’ve hit the wall. Your client projects are starting to feel like assembly-line carbon copies, and the 'basic app' trap is killing your hourly rate. You’re spending more time debugging boilerplate than solving actual business problems, while the industry shifts toward autonomous, AI-driven development workflows that threaten to leave legacy freelancers behind.

It’s time to stop writing code and start orchestrating it. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural blueprints and Claude Code for execution, you move beyond the limitations of manual development. This isn't just about speed; it's about shifting your mindset from a builder to an architect, ensuring your output is sophisticated, scalable, and impossible to replicate with standard AI tools.

steps.title: Architectural Blueprinting
steps.description: Define the app's structural core and data relationships before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular, scalable architecture for a [Type of SaaS]. Focus on decoupling the frontend from the backend services, ensuring high cohesion and low coupling. Provide the directory structure.

steps.title: Component-Driven UI Generation
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define the design system tokens and component interfaces to maintain visual consistency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a design system specification using Tailwind CSS. Define tokens for typography, spacing, and color palettes that prioritize accessibility and clean, modern aesthetic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply design system tokens to existing components'

steps.title: Logic Injection via Claude Code
steps.description: Implement complex business logic by feeding your architectural specs directly into the Claude Code terminal.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft the business logic requirements for user authentication and subscription management, including edge cases for role-based access control.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement RBAC based on the provided architecture requirements'

steps.title: Test-Driven AI Development
steps.description: Force the AI to write tests before implementation to ensure code integrity and prevent regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of comprehensive unit tests for the core business logic services defined in Step 3.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'write jest tests for all service modules before implementation'

steps.title: Optimization and Refactoring
steps.description: Analyze the codebase for performance bottlenecks and refactor for maintainability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for potential performance bottlenecks in database queries and API response times. Suggest specific refactoring patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'optimize database queries and refactor redundant logic'

pro_tips: Treat your Antigravity prompts as 'system instructions'—the more technical the constraints, the cleaner the resulting code.
Always run Claude Code in a clean environment to prevent context bloat from old, unused dependencies.
Use 'dry-run' flags whenever possible to review the diffs Claude Code proposes before full commit.
Store your successful architectural prompts in a private library for rapid reuse across different client projects.
Focus on 'primitive obsession'—instruct your AI to use strong typing and custom domain objects rather than basic arrays.
content.next_steps: Once your first module is live, use the CLI audit tools in Claude Code to verify your security posture and deploy to your staging environment for immediate client feedback.
content.conversion_block: Stop settling for basic. Elevate your freelance career by mastering the AI-first development stack. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
